claim: Chris Cornell, frontman of Soundgarden and Audioslave, was found unconscious at approximately 12:15 a.m. on May 18, 2017 in his room at the MGM Grand Detroit. He was pronounced dead at 1:30 a.m., age 52. The Wayne County Medical Examiner ruled his death a suicide by hanging. Police reviewed hotel surveillance footage showing no one entering or exiting the suite after his bodyguard left at 11:35 p.m. Cornell's toxicology report identified lorazepam, pseudoephedrine, naloxone, butalbital, and caffeine in his system.

sources.label: Wikipedia — “Chris Cornell”
sources.quote: the Wayne County Medical Examiner confirmed that the cause of death was hanging and determined the manner of death to be suicide

sources.label: CBS News Detroit — “Chris Cornell Found Dead After Show In Detroit”
tafsir_7, Instagram reel DWyiPW5jM0b (Apr 6, 2026) — surfacing source

notes: Cornell and Bennington were close friends. Cornell died May 18, 2017; Bennington died July 20, 2017 — on what would have been Cornell's 53rd birthday. The reel uses these adjacencies as part of the framing; this record documents the manner-of-death ruling as it stands.
